claim: On November 17, 2023, OpenAI's nonprofit board of directors — Ilya Sutskever (chief scientist), Adam D'Angelo (CEO of Quora), Tasha McCauley, and Helen Toner — voted to remove co-founder Sam Altman as chief executive, stating that Altman 'was not consistently candid in his communications with the board, hindering its ability to exercise its responsibilities.' Mira Murati was named interim CEO. Following pressure from OpenAI employees and major investor Microsoft, Altman was reinstated on November 22, 2023; the board was restructured, and only D'Angelo of the original four members remained.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “Removal of Sam Altman from OpenAI”
sources.quote: On November 17, 2023, OpenAI's board of directors ousted co-founder and chief executive Sam Altman

sources.label: OpenAI — “OpenAI announces leadership transition” (Nov 17, 2023 press release)
Helen Toner — public statements about the board's rationale (subsequent interviews and Senate testimony)
CNN — “How OpenAI so royally screwed up the Sam Altman firing” (Nov 19, 2023)
Ronan Farrow / Andrew Marantz, Instagram reel DYFn1LbvhP_ (May 8, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: The board's specific reasoning was disputed publicly by Altman and by Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, who said he was never given clarity on the reason. Subsequent reporting (notably the Farrow/Marantz New Yorker investigation — see 2026-farrow-altman-001) describes withheld information about ChatGPT's release, Altman's ownership of the OpenAI startup fund, and what Toner characterized as 'inaccurate information about the small number of formal safety processes' the company had in place.
